meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Stop Building, Start Selling
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of unfinished projects. Learn how to validate your SaaS idea using professional pricing strategies and AI-driven development tools.
content.answer_block: You can't finish projects because you lack market feedback. By implementing a 'Value-Based' pricing model early, you force yourself to solve a high-stakes problem for a specific persona. Use Antigravity to scaffold your MVP and Claude Code to iterate based on real revenue signals, not just code perfection.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfectly engineered' apps that nobody ever bought. You feel the weight of unfinished repositories and the stagnation of feature creep, but the real villain isn't your coding ability—it's your lack of a commercial feedback loop. When you don't have a price tag, you don't have a product; you have a hobby.

To break this cycle, we shift from 'building features' to 'selling outcomes.' By establishing a pricing model before the first database migration, you align your development efforts with actual market demand. We will use modern AI agents to transform your abstract ideas into a revenue-generating MVP that forces you to finish what you start.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Stop charging by the hour or effort. Identify the specific metric your customer values (e.g., leads generated, time saved). This creates the anchor for your pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS pricing consultant. Based on a target audience of [Insert Target Persona], generate three value-based pricing tiers that emphasize ROI over feature lists.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze these 3 pricing tiers and suggest the one with the highest conversion potential for a B2B MVP'.

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build only the core functionality that supports your highest pricing tier. Eliminate all 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean architecture for a SaaS MVP focusing strictly on [Core Feature]. Include a Stripe integration scaffold for the pricing tiers identified in step 1.

steps.title: Implement Revenue-First Routing
steps.description: Force your application to gate features behind the pricing logic immediately. This ensures your code is always tied to a transaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function for my application that checks user subscription status against the database before executing core API requests.

steps.title: Automate Feedback Loops
steps.description: Integrate a simple feedback mechanism so that when a user hits a paywall or cancels, you receive actionable insights directly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that captures user exit intent on the pricing page and logs the data to a Google Sheet for manual follow-up.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a webhook to capture pricing page exits and format them into a CSV log.'

steps.title: Iterate via Revenue Signals
steps.description: Use Claude Code to refactor based on where users drop off, not where you want to add code. If they aren't paying, the feature is dead weight.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current user drop-off rate on the pricing page and suggest 3 UI/UX changes to increase conversion.

pro_tips: Always price your MVP 20% higher than your gut feeling; if nobody complains, you are underpricing.
Never build a dashboard until you have at least 5 paying users who need to see their data.
The 'Pricing Page' is your most important feature—build it before you build the core product logic.
Use 'Founder-Led Sales' to validate your price manually before automating the payment flow.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to build, ask yourself if a customer would pay $50 just for that specific toggle.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live and accepting payments, transition from building to aggressive cold outreach. Spend 80% of your time on sales and only 20% on product refinement based on actual customer requests.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished code collect dust. By aligning your pricing with customer value, you turn development into a profitable engine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
